label: Getting around
items: New bottom navigation bar on your phone — Home, Plants, Doctor, Planner and Tools are one thumb-tap away, and Plant Doctor no longer hides two menus deep
The side menu is now grouped the way you think — Garden, Plan, AI & Tools — with a calmer highlight showing where you are
The top bar now wears Rhozly's deep-green gradient
One dashboard instead of two — the old Overview tab is now part of Home; flip the Simple/Detailed toggle for the Daily Brief, the full task list, and the stat wall
Only one setup card at a time — the Getting Started checklist, quiz reminder, notification and install prompts now take turns instead of stacking up
The weekly snapshot hides empty stats and shows each day as tappable coloured dots instead of a row of numbers

label: The Shed
items: One tidy toolbar instead of five stacked filter rows — search, Home/Favourites, Active/Archived, and a Filters button that shows how many filters you've actually applied
Calmer plant cards — the six tiny icons now live in a neat ⋯ menu; your favourite heart and Assign stay one tap away
Plants without a photo get their own coloured initial tile — same plant family, same colour — instead of everyone sharing the same stock forest photo
Status chips swapped emoji for proper icons that match the rest of Rhozly

label: Design & polish
items: Pop-ups, panels and toasts now ease in smoothly instead of snapping onto the screen (and still appear instantly if your device's Reduce Motion setting is on)
Crisper text everywhere — headings now render in their true typeface weights
Faster first load and fully offline-ready fonts — Rhozly no longer waits on Google Fonts
Confirmation toasts now match Rhozly's look instead of a plain browser default
Your phone's status bar and browser tab now show Rhozly's deep green — and the tab is finally titled Rhozly
Completing a task now scatters a little burst of leaves from your fingertip — Rhozly's way of saying nice work
Your plant grid cascades in card-by-card instead of appearing all at once
